title: Melanoseris filicina
taxonomy: Melanoseris filicina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is a member of the genus Melanoseris, which is a group of plants endemic to the Sichuan and Yunnan provinces of China. The species Melanoseris filicina is further classified as a dicotyledonous plant, characterized by having two cotyledons in its seedling stage. Within the family Asteraceae, Melanoseris filicina is placed in the subfamily Cichorioideae, which includes various dandelion-like and thistle-like plants. The plant's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

The plant's extracts are also believed to have antiviral and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to its potential health benefits.
